CREATE TABLE users (id Int32, name String, email String);
SELECT * FROM users WHERE name ILIKE 'alice';
SELECT * FROM users WHERE name ILIKE '%BOB%';
SELECT * FROM users WHERE name ILIKE 'C%';
SELECT * FROM users WHERE email ILIKE '%@example.com';
CREATE TABLE orders (id Int32, user_id Int32, description String);
INSERT INTO orders VALUES (1, 1, 'First Order'), (2, 1, 'ALICE order'), (3, 2, 'Bobs order');
SELECT u.name, o.description FROM users AS u INNER JOIN orders AS o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE o.description ILIKE 'order';
SELECT u.name, o.description FROM users AS u LEFT JOIN orders AS o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE u.name ILIKE 'CHARLIE' AND o.description ILIKE '%First%';
WITH (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE name ILIKE 'a%') AS count_a_users SELECT count_a_users;
CREATE TABLE documents (doc_id Int32, title String, content String);
INSERT INTO documents VALUES (1, 'Introduction to SQL', 'Learn SQL with us'), (2, 'Advanced SQL Techniques', 'Explore advanced SQL functions');
SELECT * FROM documents WHERE title ILIKE '%SQL%' OR content ILIKE '%sql%';
SELECT DISTINCT u.name FROM users AS u WHERE u.name ILIKE ANY ARRAY['%liCe%', '%bob%'];
SELECT u.name, d.title FROM users AS u JOIN documents AS d ON d.doc_id = u.id WHERE d.title ILIKE 'advanced %';
